what_it_does: Defines a small dataflow node that title-cases the string received on its 'text' input port and returns it on its 'text' output port.
observations: Module-level constants declare ORIGIN, VERSION, KIND, PORTS_IN, PORTS_OUT, and CONFIG.
PORTS_IN and PORTS_OUT both expose a single 'text' field typed as 'Text'.
CONFIG is an empty dict with a comment stating there are no editable fields.
The run function is a one-liner that calls str(...).title() on the input value.
ORIGIN is set to 'system' and described as 'brain-written (self-grown)' in a comment.
standouts: Extremely minimal pure transform implemented as a single str.title() call.
Self-describing node contract via top-level PORTS_IN/PORTS_OUT constants.
Provenance/version metadata embedded at module scope.
conventions: Uses module-level constants to declare node kind, version, origin, and port schema.
Implements the run(inputs, config) signature expected by the surrounding node system.
Uses Python's built-in str.title() for capitalization.
concerns: str.title() capitalizes letters after apostrophes, which can produce incorrect title casing for words like 'they're' or 'o'clock'.
No validation that inputs['text'] is actually a string.
No docstring or inline explanation of the function's behavior.
No handling for non-string, None, or missing input beyond defaulting to empty string.
notes: A self-describing, configuration-free text node that title-cases its input in one line.
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the port contract says it transforms 'Text' to 'Text', and the implementation title-cases the input text.
intention_signals: Expose a reusable, stateless text-formatting node that can be wired into a larger graph of processing nodes.
contribution: A deterministic, self-documenting title-case transform node with an explicit input/output contract and provenance metadata, suitable for wiring into a node graph without configuration.
summary_for_embedding: company/nodes/titlecase.py defines a configuration-free dataflow node that title-cases the 'text' input and returns it as 'text' output.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a reusable, pure text-transform node that normalizes incoming strings to title case within a node-based processing system.

symbols.does: Title-cases the input text string and returns it.
symbols.description: The run function expects an inputs dict containing a 'text' key and an unused config dict. It retrieves the value, defaults to an empty string, converts to str, applies Python's str.title(), and returns the result.
